content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive view of the project. Let me compile my findings into a detailed report.

## ASP.NET MVC Project Exploration Report

### Summary
The WebIde.Frontend project is a brutalist-styled competitive coding platform built with ASP.NET Core MVC, Entity Framework Core with PostgreSQL, and Tailwind CSS. The project currently implements **read-only** functionality with Index and Details views for all entities, with **NO Create/Edit/Delete (CRUD) operations** implemented yet.

---

### 1. CONTROLLER ANALYSIS: What Actions Exist?

#### ProblemController.cs
- **Route**: `[Route("problems")]`
- **Actions**:
  - `Index(string? sort)` — Lists all problems with sorting (difficulty, title, acceptance rate)
  - `Details(int id)` — Shows problem details with test cases and submissions
- **No CRUD**: No Create, Edit, Delete, Update actions

#### UserController.cs
- **Route**: `[Route("users")]`
- **Actions**:
  - `Index()` — Lists all users with roles and submission counts
  - `Details(int id)` — Shows user profile, stats, and submission history
- **No CRUD**: No Create, Edit, Delete, Update actions

#### TagController.cs
- **Route**: Default (no explicit route attribute)
- **Actions**:
  - `Index()` — Lists all tags
  - `Details(int id)` — Shows tag details
- **No CRUD**: No Create, Edit, Delete, Update actions

#### ProblemSetController.cs
- **Route**: Default (no explicit route attribute)
- **Actions**:
  - `Index()` — Lists all problem sets
  - `Details(int id)` — Shows problem set details
- **No CRUD**: No Create, Edit, Delete, Update actions

#### OrganizationController.cs
- **Route**: `[Route("orgs")]`
- **Actions**:
  - `Index()` — Lists all organizations
  - `Details(int id)` — Shows organization details
- **No CRUD**: No Create, Edit, Delete, Update actions

#### SubmissionController.cs
- **Route**: `[Route("submissions")]`
- **Actions**:
  - `Index(string? sort)` — Lists all submissions with sorting (date, score, status)
  - `Details(int id)` — Shows submission details
- **No CRUD**: No Create, Edit, Delete, Update actions

---

### 2. LAYOUT & VIEW STRUCTURE

#### _Layout.cshtml
**Brutalist Design System:**
- Fixed top navigation bar (80px height with 4px black border, sharp drop shadow)
- Tailwind CDN with custom design tokens:
  - Primary: Blue (#2752c7)
  - Secondary: Red (#b60055)
  - Tertiary: Green (#106b00)
  - Border radius: 0px (pure brutalism, except `full` for circles)
  - Fonts: Space Grotesk (headlines), Inter (body)
- Top nav includes: PROBLEMS, LEADERBOARD, ORGS, TAGS, SUBMISSIONS links
- Footer: Black background with copyright and quick links
- No authentication UI (no login/logout visible)

#### _Sidebar.cshtml
- Left sidebar (64px width, hidden on mobile, visible lg+)
- User identity block (hardcoded ROOT_USER for now)
- Navigation: DASHBOARD, PRACTICE, COMPETE, STATS, ORGS, TAGS
- Pro upgrade button
- Border-based icon buttons using Material Symbols

#### View Patterns (Problem/Index.cshtml, User/Index.cshtml)
- **Breadcrumb navigation** at top
- **Stats cards** with colored backgrounds (primary, secondary, tertiary)
- **Filter sidebar** (Problem/Index only)
- **Data table** with:
  - Black header row with white text
  - Hover states (light background)
  - Action buttons (VIEW, SOLVE, PROFILE)
  - Sortable columns with ▲/▼ indicators
- **Grid layouts**: Responsive (1 col mobile, multiple cols on larger screens)
- **No form inputs**: Read-only display only

---

### 3. MODEL STRUCTURE & VALIDATION

#### Problem.cs
```csharp
public class Problem {
    [Key] public int Id { get; set; }
    public required string Title { get; set; }
    public required string Description { get; set; }
    public DifficultyLevel Difficulty { get; set; }
    public int TimeLimitMs { get; set; }
    public int MemoryLimitKb { get; set; }
    public DateTime CreatedAt { get; set; }
    public required string AuthorUsername { get; set; }
    public virtual ICollection<TestCase> TestCases { get; set; }
    public virtual ICollection<Tag> Tags { get; set; }
    public virtual ICollection<Submission> Submissions { get; set; }
}
```
**Validation**: `required` keyword on string fields (C# 11 nullable reference type validation)

#### User.cs
```csharp
public class User {
    [Key] public int Id { get; set; }
    public required string Username { get; set; }
    public required string Email { get; set; }
    public required string DisplayName { get; set; }
    public UserRole Role { get; set; }
    public DateTime RegisteredAt { get; set; }
    public virtual ICollection<Submission> Submissions { get; set; }
    public virtual ICollection<Organization> Organizations { get; set; }
}
```
**Validation**: `required` on string fields; UserRole enum (Admin, Instructor, Student)

#### Tag.cs
```csharp
public class Tag {
    [Key] public int Id { get; set; }
    public required string Name { get; set; }
```

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive view of the project. Let me compile my findings into a detailed report.

## ASP.NET MVC Project Exploration Report

### Summary
The WebIde.Frontend project is a brutalist-styled competitive coding platform built with ASP.NET Core MVC, Entity Framework Core with PostgreSQL, and Tailwind CSS. The project currently implements **read-only** functionality with Index and Details views for all entities, with **NO Create/Edit/Delete (CRUD) operations** implemented yet.

---

### 1. CONTROLLER ANALYSIS: What Actions Exist?

#### ProblemController.cs
- **Route**: `[Route("problems")]`
- **Actions**:
  - `Index(string? sort)` — Lists all problems with sorting (difficulty, title, acceptance rate)
  - `Details(int id)` — Shows problem details with test cases and submissions
- **No CRUD**: No Create, Edit, Delete, Update actions

#### UserController.cs
- **Route**: `[Route("users")]`
- **Actions**:
  - `Index()` — Lists all users with roles and submission counts
  - `Details(int id)` — Shows user profile, stats, and submission history
- **No CRUD**: No Create, Edit, Delete, Update actions

#### TagController.cs
- **Route**: Default (no explicit route attribute)
- **Actions**:
  - `Index()` — Lists all tags
  - `Details(int id)` — Shows tag details
- **No CRUD**: No Create, Edit, Delete, Update actions

#### ProblemSetController.cs
- **Route**: Default (no explicit route attribute)
- **Actions**:
  - `Index()` — Lists all problem sets
  - `Details(int id)` — Shows problem set details
- **No CRUD**: No Create, Edit, Delete, Update actions

#### OrganizationController.cs
- **Route**: `[Route("orgs")]`
- **Actions**:
  - `Index()` — Lists all organizations
  - `Details(int id)` — Shows organization details
- **No CRUD**: No Create, Edit, Delete, Update actions

#### SubmissionController.cs
- **Route**: `[Route("submissions")]`
- **Actions**:
  - `Index(string? sort)` — Lists all submissions with sorting (date, score, status)
  - `Details(int id)` — Shows submission details
- **No CRUD**: No Create, Edit, Delete, Update actions

---

### 2. LAYOUT & VIEW STRUCTURE

#### _Layout.cshtml
**Brutalist Design System:**
- Fixed top navigation bar (80px height with 4px black border, sharp drop shadow)
- Tailwind CDN with custom design tokens:
  - Primary: Blue (#2752c7)
  - Secondary: Red (#b60055)
  - Tertiary: Green (#106b00)
  - Border radius: 0px (pure brutalism, except `full` for circles)
  - Fonts: Space Grotesk (headlines), Inter (body)
- Top nav includes: PROBLEMS, LEADERBOARD, ORGS, TAGS, SUBMISSIONS links
- Footer: Black background with copyright and quick links
- No authentication UI (no login/logout visible)

#### _Sidebar.cshtml
- Left sidebar (64px width, hidden on mobile, visible lg+)
- User identity block (hardcoded ROOT_USER for now)
- Navigation: DASHBOARD, PRACTICE, COMPETE, STATS, ORGS, TAGS
- Pro upgrade button
- Border-based icon buttons using Material Symbols

#### View Patterns (Problem/Index.cshtml, User/Index.cshtml)
- **Breadcrumb navigation** at top
- **Stats cards** with colored backgrounds (primary, secondary, tertiary)
- **Filter sidebar** (Problem/Index only)
- **Data table** with:
  - Black header row with white text
  - Hover states (light background)
  - Action buttons (VIEW, SOLVE, PROFILE)
  - Sortable columns with ▲/▼ indicators
- **Grid layouts**: Responsive (1 col mobile, multiple cols on larger screens)
- **No form inputs**: Read-only display only

---

### 3. MODEL STRUCTURE & VALIDATION

#### Problem.cs
```csharp
public class Problem {
    [Key] public int Id { get; set; }
    public required string Title { get; set; }
    public required string Description { get; set; }

    public int MemoryLimitKb { get; set; }

    public required string AuthorUsername { get; set; }
    public virtual ICollection<TestCase> TestCases { get; set; }
    public virtual ICollection<Tag> Tags { get; set; }
    public virtual ICollection<Submission> Submissions { get; set; }
}
```
**Validation**: `required` keyword on string fields (C# 11 nullable reference type validation)

#### User.cs

Now I have a very thorough understanding of the project. Let me summarize what needs to be done for Lab 4 and ask the user the key clarifying questions before building the final plan.

**Lab 4 Requirements:**
1. Full CRUD for all entities (2 pts)
   - Every list page needs AJAX search
2. Autocomplete dropdown with AJAX (2 pts)
3. Client-side + server-side validation (1 pt)
   - Client-side triggers on blur
4. Advanced JavaScript animations (1 pt)
5. Date/time control via partial view (1 pt)
   - Works with hr+en culture
   - No native browser datepicker
   - Custom or JS plugin

Before I finalize the implementation plan, I need to clarify a few things: which entities actually need full CRUD coverage since the project has several (Problem, User, Tag, ProblemSet, Organization, Submission), whether we should implement soft delete with a DeletedAt field or just hard delete, and if we should use separate CreateModel/EditModel classes as the lab suggests or keep things simpler.

For the relationships, I'm trying to figure out which ones would benefit most from autocomplete dropdowns—Problem to Tags seems natural since it's many-to-many, but ProblemSet to Organization and Organization to Members are also candidates. I also need to decide if all the date fields across Problem, User, ProblemSet, and Submission should use the custom date picker, or if we can be selective about which ones get that treatment.

On the date picker itself, I'm weighing whether to use a maintained library like Flatpickr or build something custom that fits the brutalist aesthetic better. For AJAX search on index pages, I'm considering whether to filter on the server side or load everything client-side and filter locally. TestCases are another decision point—should they be managed inline within the Problem form or as a separate controller? And finally, I need to clarify the scope: does the professor expect all eight entities fully implemented, or just the core ones like Problem, User, Tag, Organization, and ProblemSet?

I should focus on asking the most critical clarifying questions without overwhelming them.

questions.question: Which entities need full CRUD? The project has Problem, User, Tag, Organization, ProblemSet, Submission, TestCase. Submissions and ExecutionResults are typically auto-generated by a judge — should we include them?

options.label: Main 5 + TestCase (Recommended)
options.description: Full CRUD for Problem, User, Tag, Organization, ProblemSet — plus TestCase managed inline within Problem forms. Skip Submission/ExecutionResult (auto-generated by judge).

options.label: All 7 entities
options.description: CRUD for every entity including Submission (manual create) and TestCase. Most work, highest coverage.

options.label: Main 5 only
options.description: Problem, User, Tag, Organization, ProblemSet. Exclude TestCase and Submission entirely.

questions.question: Should we implement soft delete (add a DeletedAt timestamp to entities and filter it out everywhere) or hard delete (physically remove from DB)?

options.label: Soft delete (Recommended)
options.description: Add DeletedAt nullable field via migration. More professional, shows understanding of the concept described in the lab. All queries filter WHERE DeletedAt IS NULL.

options.label: Hard delete
options.description: Physically remove records with EF Remove(). Simpler, no migration needed. Be careful with cascade constraints.

questions.question: For the required AJAX autocomplete dropdown, which relationship is the most natural showcase for this app?

options.label: Tags on Problem form (Recommended)
options.description: When creating/editing a Problem, search and attach Tags via autocomplete. Natural many-to-many relationship, good for demonstrating multi-select autocomplete.

options.label: Organization on ProblemSet form
options.description: When creating/editing a ProblemSet, search and select the owning Organization. Clean 1-N foreign key, simpler to implement.

options.label: Both
options.description: Implement autocomplete for Tags on Problem form AND Organization on ProblemSet form. More work but shows the reusable partial view pattern from the lab.

questions.question: For the custom datepicker (required by lab — no native browser input[type=date]), which approach fits the brutalist design?

options.label: Flatpickr plugin (Recommended)
options.description: Lightweight, maintained JS plugin. Highly customizable CSS — we can strip all rounded corners and apply brutalist border styling. Supports hr/en locales natively.

options.label: Custom built
options.description: Fully hand-coded JS calendar rendered in a partial view. Maximum control over design, but significantly more JS to write and test.

content.text:
Perfect! Here's the complete analysis of the data model relationships:

## Submission.cs Analysis

**ForeignKey Attributes:**
- `UserId` (line 18): int FK property with `[ForeignKey("UserId")]` attribute (line 19) pointing to navigation property `User`
- `ProblemId` (line 21): int FK property with `[ForeignKey("ProblemId")]` attribute (line 22) pointing to navigation property `Problem`
- `ExecutionResultId` (line 24): int? (nullable) FK property with `[ForeignKey("ExecutionResultId")]` attribute (line 25) pointing to navigation property `ExecutionResult?`

All three are proper int FK properties (or int? for ExecutionResultId), not just navigation properties.

## Relationship Summary

| Relationship | Type | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| **Submission ↔ ExecutionResult** | **1:0 or 1:1** | ExecutionResultId is `int?` (nullable), ExecutionResult is optional (`ExecutionResult?`). One Submission may have zero or one ExecutionResult |
| **Submission ↔ Problem** | **N:1** | ProblemId is required `int`, multiple Submissions can reference same Problem |
| **Submission ↔ User** | **N:1** | UserId is required `int`, multiple Submissions can reference same User |

## ExecutionResult.cs

ExecutionResult is a simple entity with:
- `Id` (PK)
- `Stdout` (required string)
- `Stderr` (required string)
- `ExitCode` (int)
- `TimedOut` (bool)
- `MemoryExceeded` (bool)

**Note:** ExecutionResult has NO navigation property back to Submission. The relationship is one-directional from Submission side only.

## TestCase.cs Fields

- `Id` (PK)
- `InputArgs` (required string)
- `ExpectedOutput` (required string)
- `IsSample` (bool)
- `OrderIndex` (int)
- `Points` (int)
- `ProblemId` (int FK) with `[ForeignKey("ProblemId")]`
- `Problem` (navigation property)

Relationship: **TestCase ↔ Problem** is **N:1** (many test cases per problem)

## DbContext Configuration

The `WebIdeDbContext.OnModelCreating()` method configures:
- **Problem ↔ Tag**: N:N with explicit join table "ProblemTags"
- **ProblemSet ↔ Problem**: N:N with explicit join table "ProblemSetProblems"
- **Organization ↔ User**: N:N with explicit join table "OrganizationMembers"

No explicit fluent API configuration for Submission, ExecutionResult, or TestCase relationships—they rely on data annotations.

input.content:
# Lab 4 Implementation Plan

## Context

The WebIde project is a LeetCode-clone competitive coding platform built with ASP.NET MVC (.NET 10), EF Core 9, PostgreSQL, and Tailwind CSS (brutalist design). Lab 3 completed the EF Core DAL + read-only views. Lab 4 adds full CRUD, AJAX search, autocomplete dropdown, client+server validation, custom datepicker (Flatpickr), and advanced JS animations.

Currently: zero CRUD actions exist — all controllers are read-only, repositories have no write methods, no form views exist.

---

## Entities and CRUD Scope

| Entity | CRUD | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Problem | Full CRUD | Autocomplete for Tags (multi-select) |
| User | Full CRUD | |
| Tag | Full CRUD | Simple (Name only) |
| Organization | Full CRUD | |
| ProblemSet | Full CRUD | Autocomplete for Organization FK |
| Submission | Full CRUD | Autocomplete for Problem + User FKs |
| TestCase | Full CRUD | Separate pages, linked from Problem Detail |
| ExecutionResult | Create + Read | Factual auto-generated record — no Edit/Delete |

---

## Key Decisions

- **Soft delete**: Add `DeletedAt DateTime?` to Problem, User, Tag, Organization, ProblemSet, Submission, TestCase. All GetAll queries filter `WHERE DeletedAt IS NULL`.
- **ViewModels**: Separate `XCreateModel` and `XEditModel` per entity in `WebIde.Frontend/Models/`.
- **Validation**: Data annotations on ViewModels. Server: `ModelState.IsValid`. Client: jQuery Unobtrusive Validation triggering on `blur` (not on submit).
- **Autocomplete**: Tags on Problem form (multi-select), Organization on ProblemSet form (single-select), Problem + User on Submission form. Reusable `_AutocompleteDropdown.cshtml` partial.
- **Datepicker**: Flatpickr plugin via CDN. Brutalist-styled (no rounded corners, black border). Partial view `_DatePicker.cshtml` applied everywhere dates are used (Problem.CreatedAt, User.RegisteredAt, ProblemSet.CreatedAt, Submission.SubmittedAt).
- **AJAX search**: Each Index page queries `/{entity}/search?q=` endpoint that returns JSON. JS replaces table rows with animated fade-in.
- **Advanced JS**: Row stagger animation on page load, animated delete confirmation modal (no browser `confirm()`), AJAX loading skeleton, smooth autocomplete dropdown.

---

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Soft Delete Migration

**Files to modify:**
- `WebIde.Model/Problem.cs` — add `public DateTime? DeletedAt { get; set; }`
- `WebIde.Model/User.cs` — same
- `WebIde.Model/Tag.cs` — same
- `WebIde.Model/Organization.cs` — same
- `WebIde.Model/ProblemSet.cs` — same
- `WebIde.Model/Submission.cs` — same
- `WebIde.Model/TestCase.cs` — same

Then run:
```
cd WebIde.Frontend
dotnet ef migrations add AddSoftDelete --project ../WebIde.DAL --startup-project .
dotnet ef database update --project ../WebIde.DAL --startup-project .
```

---

### Step 2: ViewModels

Create `WebIde.Frontend/Models/` directory with one file per entity:

**ProblemCreateModel / ProblemEditModel**
```csharp
public class ProblemCreateModel {
    [Required] [StringLength(200, MinimumLength = 3)] public string Title { get; set; }
    [Required] [StringLength(10000, MinimumLength = 10)] public string Description { get; set; }
    public DifficultyLevel Difficulty { get; set; }
    [Range(100, 10000)] public int TimeLimitMs { get; set; }
    [Range(1024, 524288)] public int MemoryLimitKb { get; set; }
    [Required] public string AuthorUsername { get; set; }
    public DateTime CreatedAt { get; set; }
    public List<int> TagIds { get; set; } = new(); // from autocomplete
}
```
EditModel same fields + `Id`.

Similar pattern for: UserCreateModel/EditModel, TagCreateModel/EditModel, OrganizationCreateModel/EditModel, ProblemSetCreateModel/EditModel, SubmissionCreateModel/EditModel, TestCaseCreateModel/EditModel, ExecutionResultCreateModel.

---

### Step 3: Repository Write Methods

Add to each repository (example for ProblemRepository):
```csharp
public void Add(Problem entity) { _db.Problems.Add(entity); _db.SaveChanges(); }
public void Update() { _db.SaveChanges(); }
public void SoftDelete(int id) {
    var e = _db.Problems.Find(id);
    if (e != null) { e.DeletedAt = DateTime.UtcNow; _db.SaveChanges(); }
}
public List<Problem> Search(string q) =>
    _db.Problems
        .Where(p => p.DeletedAt == null && p.Title.Contains(q))
        .Take(20).ToList();
```

**Also update all existing `GetAll()` and `GetById()` to filter `DeletedAt == null`.**

Files to modify:
- `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/ProblemRepository.cs`
- `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/UserRepository.cs`
- `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/TagRepository.cs`
- `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/OrganizationRepository.cs`
- `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/ProblemSetRepository.cs`
- `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/SubmissionRepository.cs`
- Add new: `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/TestCaseRepository.cs`
- Add new: `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/ExecutionResultRepository.cs`

---

### Step 4: Shared Partial Views

#### `Views/Shared/_DatePicker.cshtml`
Flatpickr partial. Parameters: field name, display label, current value (nullable), is-required.
- Renders a text input wired to Flatpickr with locale from `System.Globalization.CultureInfo.CurrentCulture`
- Hidden `input[type=hidden]` stores UTC ISO value for model binding
- Brutalist styling: 2px black border, no border-radius, monospace font

#### `Views/Shared/_AutocompleteDropdown.cshtml`
Reusable autocomplete partial. Parameters: field name (for hidden ID), display label, search endpoint URL, initial display text (for edit form), is-multi (bool for Tag multi-select vs single FK select).
- Text input shows user-readable name
- Hidden input(s) store selected ID(s)
- AJAX on `keyup` (debounced 300ms) → calls search endpoint → renders dropdown list
- Selecting an item fills the hidden input and display text
- Brutalist dropdown: black border, white background, hover = black bg + white text

#### `Views/Shared/_DeleteModal.cshtml`
Reusable animated delete confirmation modal. Used from all Index/Details pages.

---

### Step 5: Flatpickr + Localization Setup

**`WebIde.Frontend/Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml`** — add in `<head>`:
```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/flatpickr/dist/flatpickr.min.css">
```
Add before `</body>`:
```html
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/flatpickr"></script>
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/flatpickr/dist/l10n/hr.js"></script>
<partial name="_ValidationScriptsPartial" />
```

**`WebIde.Frontend/Program.cs`** — add before `app.MapControllerRoute`:
```csharp
var supportedCultures = new[] { new CultureInfo("hr"), new CultureInfo("en-US") };
app.UseRequestLocalization(new RequestLocalizationOptions {
    DefaultRequestCulture = new RequestCulture("hr"),
    SupportedCultures = supportedCultures,
    SupportedUICultures = supportedCultures
});
```

---

### Step 6: CRUD Controllers + Views (entity by entity)

Implement in this order (simple to complex): **Tag → Organization → User → Problem → ProblemSet → Submission → TestCase → ExecutionResult**

For each entity, the controller gets:
```csharp
[HttpGet("create")]
public IActionResult Create() { /* populate ViewBag if needed, return View() */ }

[HttpPost("create")]
public IActionResult Create(XCreateModel model) {
    if (!ModelState.IsValid) return View(model);
    // map model → entity
    _repo.Add(entity);
    return RedirectToAction(nameof(Index));
}

[HttpGet("{id}/edit"), ActionName("Edit")]
public IActionResult EditGet(int id) { /* fetch entity, map to EditModel, return View */ }

[HttpPost("{id}/edit"), ActionName("Edit")]
public async Task<IActionResult> EditPost(int id, XEditModel model) {
    if (!ModelState.IsValid) return View(model);
    // fetch entity, map model fields → entity, Update()
    return RedirectToAction(nameof(Index));
}

[HttpPost("{id}/delete")]
public IActionResult Delete(int id) {
    _repo.SoftDelete(id);
    return RedirectToAction(nameof(Index));
}

[HttpGet("search")]
public IActionResult Search(string q) {
    var results = _repo.Search(q ?? "");
    return Json(results.Select(x => new { x.Id, label = x.Name })); // per entity
}
```

Views per entity:
- `Views/X/Create.cshtml`
- `Views/X/Edit.cshtml`
- Update `Views/X/Index.cshtml` to add Create button, AJAX search input, Edit/Delete buttons per row

Form design: brutalist form fields (2px black border, no radius, Space Grotesk labels, monospace inputs), validation spans `<span asp-validation-for="X">`, group in `<div class="form-group">`.

---

### Step 7: AJAX Search on All Index Pages

Each Index.cshtml gets a search input + JS that:
1. Debounces `keyup` by 300ms
2. POSTs to `/entity/search?q=input.value`
3. Replaces `<tbody>` contents with rendered rows (JS template literal)
4. Animates new rows in with a stagger fade-in

```js
// site.js pattern (shared)
function initAjaxSearch(inputId, tbodyId, endpoint, rowRenderer) {
  let timer;
  document.getElementById(inputId).addEventListener('keyup', function() {
    clearTimeout(timer);
    timer = setTimeout(() => {
      fetch(`${endpoint}?q=${this.value}`)
        .then(r => r.json())
        .then(data => {
          const tbody = document.getElementById(tbodyId);
          tbody.innerHTML = data.map(rowRenderer).join('');
          // stagger animate rows
          [...tbody.querySelectorAll('tr')].forEach((tr, i) => {
            tr.style.opacity = 0;
            setTimeout(() => tr.style.transition = 'opacity 0.2s', i * 40);
            setTimeout(() => tr.style.opacity = 1, i * 40 + 10);
          });
        });
    }, 300);
  });
}
```

---

### Step 8: Client-Side Validation on Blur

In `_Layout.cshtml` after `_ValidationScriptsPartial`, add to `site.js`:
```js
// Trigger jQuery Unobtrusive Validation on blur for all form inputs
$(document).on('blur', 'input, select, textarea', function() {
    var form = $(this).closest('form');
    if (form.data('validator')) {
        form.validate().element(this);
    }
});
```

---

### Step 9: Advanced JS Animations

Add to `wwwroot/js/site.js`:
- **Page load row stagger**: On DOM ready, all `tbody tr` start at `opacity:0` and fade in sequentially (40ms gap per row)
- **Delete modal animation**: Custom modal that slides down from top, backdrop fades in, "CONFIRM DELETE" button triggers red flash before redirect
- **Autocomplete dropdown**: Slides down with CSS `transform: scaleY(0) → scaleY(1)` + `transform-origin: top`
- **AJAX loading skeleton**: During search fetch, replace tbody with 3 skeleton rows (animated grey pulse)
- **Form focus effects**: When an input gains focus, its border transitions from 2px to 3px black (brutalist "active" state)

---

### Step 10: TestCase and ExecutionResult

**TestCase** — separate CRUD controller + views:
- Route: `[Route("problems/{problemId}/testcases")]`
- All actions scoped to a parent Problem
- Index shows all TestCases for a Problem
- Create/Edit/SoftDelete

**ExecutionResult** — Create + Read:
- Route: `[Route("submissions/{submissionId}/results")]`
- Create: attach a result to a Submission
- Read: view result details (stdout, stderr, exit code)
- No Edit/SoftDelete (factual record)

Register new repositories in `Program.cs`.

---

# Lab 4 Implementation Plan

## Context

The WebIde project is a LeetCode-clone competitive coding platform built with ASP.NET MVC (.NET 10), EF Core 9, PostgreSQL, and Tailwind CSS (brutalist design). Lab 3 completed the EF Core DAL + read-only views. Lab 4 adds full CRUD, AJAX search, autocomplete dropdown, client+server validation, custom datepicker (Flatpickr), and advanced JS animations.

Currently: zero CRUD actions exist — all controllers are read-only, repositories have no write methods, no form views exist.

---

## Entities and CRUD Scope

| Entity | CRUD | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Problem | Full CRUD | Autocomplete for Tags (multi-select) |
| User | Full CRUD | |
| Tag | Full CRUD | Simple (Name only) |
| Organization | Full CRUD | |
| ProblemSet | Full CRUD | Autocomplete for Organization FK |
| Submission | Full CRUD | Autocomplete for Problem + User FKs |
| TestCase | Full CRUD | Separate pages, linked from Problem Detail |
| ExecutionResult | Create + Read | Factual auto-generated record — no Edit/Delete |

---

## Key Decisions

- **Soft delete**: Add `DeletedAt DateTime?` to Problem, User, Tag, Organization, ProblemSet, Submission, TestCase. All GetAll queries filter `WHERE DeletedAt IS NULL`.
- **ViewModels**: Separate `XCreateModel` and `XEditModel` per entity in `WebIde.Frontend/Models/`.
- **Validation**: Data annotations on ViewModels. Server: `ModelState.IsValid`. Client: jQuery Unobtrusive Validation triggering on `blur` (not on submit).
- **Autocomplete**: Tags on Problem form (multi-select), Organization on ProblemSet form (single-select), Problem + User on Submission form. Reusable `_AutocompleteDropdown.cshtml` partial.
- **Datepicker**: Flatpickr plugin via CDN. Brutalist-styled (no rounded corners, black border). Partial view `_DatePicker.cshtml` applied everywhere dates are used (Problem.CreatedAt, User.RegisteredAt, ProblemSet.CreatedAt, Submission.SubmittedAt).
- **AJAX search**: Each Index page queries `/{entity}/search?q=` endpoint that returns JSON. JS replaces table rows with animated fade-in.
- **Advanced JS**: Row stagger animation on page load, animated delete confirmation modal (no browser `confirm()`), AJAX loading skeleton, smooth autocomplete dropdown.

---

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Soft Delete Migration

**Files to modify:**
- `WebIde.Model/Problem.cs` — add `public DateTime? DeletedAt { get; set; }`
- `WebIde.Model/User.cs` — same
- `WebIde.Model/Tag.cs` — same
- `WebIde.Model/Organization.cs` — same
- `WebIde.Model/ProblemSet.cs` — same
- `WebIde.Model/Submission.cs` — same
- `WebIde.Model/TestCase.cs` — same

Then run:
```
cd WebIde.Frontend
dotnet ef migrations add AddSoftDelete --project ../WebIde.DAL --startup-project .
dotnet ef database update --project ../WebIde.DAL --startup-project .
```

---

### Step 2: ViewModels

Create `WebIde.Frontend/Models/` directory with one file per entity:

**ProblemCreateModel / ProblemEditModel**
```csharp
public class ProblemCreateModel {
    [Required] [StringLength(200, MinimumLength = 3)] public string Title { get; set; }
    [Required] [StringLength(10000, MinimumLength = 10)] public string Description { get; set; }
    public DifficultyLevel Difficulty { get; set; }
    [Range(100, 10000)] public int TimeLimitMs { get; set; }
    [Range(1024, 524288)] public int MemoryLimitKb { get; set; }
    [Required] public string AuthorUsername { get; set; }
    public DateTime CreatedAt { get; set; }
    public List<int> TagIds { get; set; } = new(); // from autocomplete
}
```
EditModel same fields + `Id`.

Similar pattern for: UserCreateModel/EditModel, TagCreateModel/EditModel, OrganizationCreateModel/EditModel, ProblemSetCreateModel/EditModel, SubmissionCreateModel/EditModel, TestCaseCreateModel/EditModel, ExecutionResultCreateModel.

---

### Step 3: Repository Write Methods

Add to each repository (example for ProblemRepository):
```csharp
public void Add(Problem entity) { _db.Problems.Add(entity); _db.SaveChanges(); }
public void Update() { _db.SaveChanges(); }
public void SoftDelete(int id) {
    var e = _db.Problems.Find(id);
    if (e != null) { e.DeletedAt = DateTime.UtcNow; _db.SaveChanges(); }
}
public List<Problem> Search(string q) =>
    _db.Problems
        .Where(p => p.DeletedAt == null && p.Title.Contains(q))
        .Take(20).ToList();
```

**Also update all existing `GetAll()` and `GetById()` to filter `DeletedAt == null`.**

Files to modify:
- `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/ProblemRepository.cs`
- `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/UserRepository.cs`
- `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/TagRepository.cs`
- `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/OrganizationRepository.cs`
- `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/ProblemSetRepository.cs`
- `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/SubmissionRepository.cs`
- Add new: `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/TestCaseRepository.cs`
- Add new: `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/ExecutionResultRepository.cs`

---

### Step 4: Shared Partial Views

#### `Views/Shared/_DatePicker.cshtml`
Flatpickr partial. Parameters: field name, display label, current value (nullable), is-required.
- Renders a text input wired to Flatpickr with locale from `System.Globalization.CultureInfo.CurrentCulture`
- Hidden `input[type=hidden]` stores UTC ISO value for model binding
- Brutalist styling: 2px black border, no border-radius, monospace font

#### `Views/Shared/_AutocompleteDropdown.cshtml`
Reusable autocomplete partial. Parameters: field name (for hidden ID), display label, search endpoint URL, initial display text (for edit form), is-multi (bool for Tag multi-select vs single FK select).
- Text input shows user-readable name
- Hidden input(s) store selected ID(s)
- AJAX on `keyup` (debounced 300ms) → calls search endpoint → renders dropdown list
- Selecting an item fills the hidden input and display text
- Brutalist dropdown: black border, white background, hover = black bg + white text

#### `Views/Shared/_DeleteModal.cshtml`
Reusable animated delete confirmation modal. Used from all Index/Details pages.

---

### Step 5: Flatpickr + Localization Setup

**`WebIde.Frontend/Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml`** — add in `<head>`:
```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/flatpickr/dist/flatpickr.min.css">
```
Add before `</body>`:
```html
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/flatpickr"></script>
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/flatpickr/dist/l10n/hr.js"></script>
<partial name="_ValidationScriptsPartial" />
```

**`WebIde.Frontend/Program.cs`** — add before `app.MapControllerRoute`:
```csharp
var supportedCultures = new[] { new CultureInfo("hr"), new CultureInfo("en-US") };
app.UseRequestLocalization(new RequestLocalizationOptions {
    DefaultRequestCulture = new RequestCulture("hr"),
    SupportedCultures = supportedCultures,
    SupportedUICultures = supportedCultures
});
```

---

### Step 6: CRUD Controllers + Views (entity by entity)

Implement in this order (simple to complex): **Tag → Organization → User → Problem → ProblemSet → Submission → TestCase → ExecutionResult**

For each entity, the controller gets:
```csharp
[HttpGet("create")]
public IActionResult Create() { /* populate ViewBag if needed, return View() */ }

[HttpPost("create")]
public IActionResult Create(XCreateModel model) {
    if (!ModelState.IsValid) return View(model);
    // map model → entity
    _repo.Add(entity);
    return RedirectToAction(nameof(Index));
}

[HttpGet("{id}/edit"), ActionName("Edit")]
public IActionResult EditGet(int id) { /* fetch entity, map to EditModel, return View */ }

[HttpPost("{id}/edit"), ActionName("Edit")]
public async Task<IActionResult> EditPost(int id, XEditModel model) {
    if (!ModelState.IsValid) return View(model);
    // fetch entity, map model fields → entity, Update()
    return RedirectToAction(nameof(Index));
}

[HttpPost("{id}/delete")]
public IActionResult Delete(int id) {
    _repo.SoftDelete(id);
    return RedirectToAction(nameof(Index));
}

[HttpGet("search")]
public IActionResult Search(string q) {
    var results = _repo.Search(q ?? "");
    return Json(results.Select(x => new { x.Id, label = x.Name })); // per entity
}
```

Views per entity:
- `Views/X/Create.cshtml`
- `Views/X/Edit.cshtml`
- Update `Views/X/Index.cshtml` to add Create button, AJAX search input, Edit/Delete buttons per row

Form design: brutalist form fields (2px black border, no radius, Space Grotesk labels, monospace inputs), validation spans `<span asp-validation-for="X">`, group in `<div class="form-group">`.

---

### Step 7: AJAX Search on All Index Pages

Each Index.cshtml gets a search input + JS that:

3. Replaces `<tbody>` contents with rendered rows (JS template literal)

```js
          const tbody = document.getElementById(tbodyId);
          tbody.innerHTML = data.map(rowRenderer).join('');
          // stagger animate rows

            setTimeout(() => tr.style.opacity = 1, i * 40 + 10);
          });
        });
    }, 300);
  });

```

---

### Step 8: Client-Side Validation on Blur

In `_Layout.cshtml` after `_ValidationScriptsPartial`, add to `site.js`:
```js

$(document).on('blur', 'input, select, textarea', function() {
    var form = $(this).closest('form');
    if (form.data('validator')) {
        form.validate().element(this);
```

---

### Step 9: Advanced JS Animations

Add to `wwwroot/js/site.js`:
- **Page load row stagger**: On DOM ready, all `tbody tr` start at `opacity:0` and fade in sequentially (40ms gap per row)
- **Delete modal animation**: Custom modal that slides down from top, backdrop fades in, "CONFIRM DELETE" button triggers red flash before redirect
- **Autocomplete dropdown**: Slides down with CSS `transform: scaleY(0) → scaleY(1)` + `transform-origin: top`
- **AJAX loading skeleton**: During search fetch, replace tbody with 3 skeleton rows (animated grey pulse)
- **Form focus effects**: When an input gains focus, its border transitions from 2px to 3px black (brutalist "active" state)

---

### Step 10: TestCase and ExecutionResult

Implement in this order (simple to complex): **Tag → Organization → User → Problem → ProblemSet → Submission → TestCase → ExecutionResult**

For each entity, the controller gets:
```csharp
[HttpGet("create")]
public IActionResult Create() { /* populate ViewBag if needed, return View() */ }

[HttpPost("create")]
public IActionResult Create(XCreateModel model) {
    if (!ModelState.IsValid) return View(model);
    // map model → entity
    _repo.Add(entity);
    return RedirectToAction(nameof(Index));
}

[HttpGet("{id}/edit"), ActionName("Edit")]
public IActionResult EditGet(int id) { /* fetch entity, map to EditModel, return View */ }

[HttpPost("{id}/edit"), ActionName("Edit")]
public async Task<IActionResult> EditPost(int id, XEditModel model) {
    if (!ModelState.IsValid) return View(model);
    // fetch entity, map model fields → entity, Update()
    return RedirectToAction(nameof(Index));
}

[HttpPost("{id}/delete")]
public IActionResult Delete(int id) {
    _repo.SoftDelete(id);
    return RedirectToAction(nameof(Index));
}

[HttpGet("search")]
public IActionResult Search(string q) {
    var results = _repo.Search(q ?? "");
    return Json(results.Select(x => new { x.Id, label = x.Name })); // per entity
}
```

Views per entity:
- `Views/X/Create.cshtml`
- `Views/X/Edit.cshtml`
- Update `Views/X/Index.cshtml` to add Create button, AJAX search input, Edit/Delete buttons per row

Form design: brutalist form fields (2px black border, no radius, Space Grotesk labels, monospace inputs), validation spans `<span asp-validation-for="X">`, group in `<div class="form-group">`.

---

### Step 7: AJAX Search on All Index Pages

Each Index.cshtml gets a search input + JS that:
1. Debounces `keyup` by 300ms
2. POSTs to `/entity/search?q=input.value`
3. Replaces `<tbody>` contents with rendered rows (JS template literal)
4. Animates new rows in with a stagger fade-in

```js
// site.js pattern (shared)
function initAjaxSearch(inputId, tbodyId, endpoint, rowRenderer) {
  let timer;
  document.getElementById(inputId).addEventListener('keyup', function() {
    clearTimeout(timer);
    timer = setTimeout(() => {
      fetch(`${endpoint}?q=${this.value}`)
        .then(r => r.json())
        .then(data => {
          const tbody = document.getElementById(tbodyId);
          tbody.innerHTML = data.map(rowRenderer).join('');
          // stagger animate rows
          [...tbody.querySelectorAll('tr')].forEach((tr, i) => {
            tr.style.opacity = 0;
            setTimeout(() => tr.style.transition = 'opacity 0.2s', i * 40);
            setTimeout(() => tr.style.opacity = 1, i * 40 + 10);
          });
        });
    }, 300);
  });
}
```

---

### Step 8: Client-Side Validation on Blur

In `_Layout.cshtml` after `_ValidationScriptsPartial`, add to `site.js`:
```js
// Trigger jQuery Unobtrusive Validation on blur for all form inputs
$(document).on('blur', 'input, select, textarea', function() {
    var form = $(this).closest('form');
    if (form.data('validator')) {
        form.validate().element(this);
    }
});
```

---

### Step 9: Advanced JS Animations

Add to `wwwroot/js/site.js`:
- **Page load row stagger**: On DOM ready, all `tbody tr` start at `opacity:0` and fade in sequentially (40ms gap per row)
- **Delete modal animation**: Custom modal that slides down from top, backdrop fades in, "CONFIRM DELETE" button triggers red flash before redirect
- **Autocomplete dropdown**: Slides down with CSS `transform: scaleY(0) → scaleY(1)` + `transform-origin: top`
- **AJAX loading skeleton**: During search fetch, replace tbody with 3 skeleton rows (animated grey pulse)
- **Form focus effects**: When an input gains focus, its border transitions from 2px to 3px black (brutalist "active" state)

---

### Step 10: TestCase and ExecutionResult

**TestCase** — separate CRUD controller + views:
- Route: `[Route("problems/{problemId}/testcases")]`
- All actions scoped to a parent Problem
- Index shows all TestCases for a Problem
- Create/Edit/SoftDelete

**ExecutionResult** — Create + Read:
- Route: `[Route("submissions/{submissionId}/results")]`
- Create: attach a result to a Submission
- Read: view result details (stdout, stderr, exit code)
- No Edit/SoftDelete (factual record)

Register new repositories in `Program.cs`.

<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net10.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
  </PropertyGroup>

      <IncludeAssets>runtime; build; native; contentfiles; analyzers; buildtransitive</IncludeAssets>
      <PrivateAssets>all</PrivateAssets>
    </PackageReference>
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>

public List<Problem> GetAll() =>
        _db.Problems
            .Include(p => p.Tags)
            .Include(p => p.Submissions)
            .OrderBy(p => p.Id)
            .ToList();

    public Problem? GetById(int id) =>
        _db.Problems
            .Include(p => p.Tags)
            .Include(p => p.TestCases)
            .Include(p => p.Submissions).ThenInclude(s => s.User)
            .FirstOrDefault(p => p.Id == id);
}

public List<ProblemSet> GetAll() =>
        _db.ProblemSets
            .Include(ps => ps.Organization)
            .Include(ps => ps.Problems)
            .OrderBy(ps => ps.OrderIndex)
            .ToList();

    public ProblemSet? GetById(int id) =>
        _db.ProblemSets
            .Include(ps => ps.Organization)
            .Include(ps => ps.Problems).ThenInclude(p => p.Tags)
            .Include(ps => ps.Problems).ThenInclude(p => p.Submissions)
            .FirstOrDefault(ps => ps.Id == id);
}

public List<Submission> GetAll() =>
        _db.Submissions
            .Include(s => s.User)
            .Include(s => s.Problem)
            .Include(s => s.ExecutionResult)
            .OrderByDescending(s => s.SubmittedAt)
            .ToList();

    public Submission? GetById(int id) =>
        _db.Submissions
            .Include(s => s.User)
            .Include(s => s.Problem)
            .Include(s => s.ExecutionResult)

content.thinking:
The issue is in `_DatePicker.cshtml` - `inputId_hidden` is being used as a C# variable name in Razor but it was only defined in a JavaScript string concatenation. In Razor, string concatenation with `_` in the variable declaration is not valid for creating a new variable. The variable `inputId` exists, but `inputId_hidden` is used in the Razor HTML (not JS). I need to fix this.


Looking at the specific lines, I can see the hidden input field is trying to reference `inputId_hidden` directly, but that variable was never properly declared in C#. The solution is to use string interpolation like `@(inputId + "_hidden")` to concatenate the variable name at runtime, or define the variable properly in the Razor code block.

content.text: Fix the `_DatePicker.cshtml` — `inputId_hidden` used as a C# variable instead of a string concat:

input.old_string:
<input type="hidden"
               name="@fieldName"
               id="@inputId_hidden"
               value="@isoValue" />
input.new_string:
<input type="hidden"
               name="@fieldName"
               id="@(inputId)_hidden"
               value="@isoValue" />

SUBMISSIONS

@{ string currentSort = ViewData["sort"]?.ToString() ?? "date-desc"; string NextDateSort() => currentSort == "date-desc" ? "date-asc" : "date-desc"; string NextScoreSort() => currentSort == "score-desc" ? "score-asc" : "score-desc"; }
@foreach (var sub in Model) { }
USER PROBLEM LANG STATUS@(currentSort == "status" ? " ▲" : "") SCORE@(currentSort == "score-desc" ? " ▼" : currentSort == "score-asc" ? " ▲" : "") TIME MEMORY SUBMITTED@(currentSort == "date-desc" ? " ▼" : currentSort == "date-asc" ? " ▲" : "") DETAIL
@sub.User.Username @sub.Problem.Title @sub.Language.ToString().ToUpper() @switch (sub.Status) { case WebIde.Model.Enums.SubmissionStatus.Accepted: ACCEPTED break; case WebIde.Model.Enums.SubmissionStatus.WrongAnswer: WRONG break; case WebIde.Model.Enums.SubmissionStatus.TimeLimitExceeded: TLE break; case WebIde.Model.Enums.SubmissionStatus.CompileError: CE break; default: @sub.Status.ToString().ToUpper() break; } @sub.Score @sub.WallTimeMs ms @sub.PeakMemoryKb KB @sub.SubmittedAt.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm") VIEW

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested full implementation of Lab 4 for a competitive coding platform (WebIde). Requirements from Lab4.md:
   - Complete CRUD support for ALL entities (Problem, User, Tag, Organization, ProblemSet, Submission, TestCase, ExecutionResult)
   - AJAX search on every Index/list page
   - Custom AJAX autocomplete dropdown (reusable partial view)
   - Client-side validation triggering on blur + server-side validation
   - Custom datepicker (date+time) via partial view — NO native browser datepicker; must use JS plugin or custom code; must work with hr/en locale
   - Advanced JavaScript animations serving the application
   - Soft delete strategy (DeletedAt timestamp)
   - Save chat log to lab-4 folder after completion

   User answered planning questions:
   - All entities need CRUD
   - Soft delete (Recommended)
   - Tags autocomplete on Problem form (multi-select), plus noted ExecutionResult has 1:0..1 relationship with Submission
   - Flatpickr plugin for datepicker
   - TestCase: separate CRUD pages (not inline); ExecutionResult: also separate CRUD for history display
   - Submission FK fields: AJAX autocomplete for BOTH User and Problem

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - ASP.NET MVC (.NET 10) with Razor views (server-rendered, NOT React/SPA)
   - Entity Framework Core 9 with Npgsql PostgreSQL provider
   - Repository pattern for data access
   - Soft delete pattern: `DeletedAt DateTime?` field, filter in all queries
   - ViewModels (XCreateModel / XEditModel) separate from entity models
   - `[ActionName("Edit")]` pattern for GET/POST same-URL actions
   - `[ValidateAntiForgeryToken]` on all POST actions
   - `ModelState.IsValid` server-side validation
   - Data annotations: `[Required]`, `[StringLength]`, `[Range]`, `[EmailAddress]`, `[RegularExpression]`
   - jQuery Unobtrusive Validation for client-side validation, triggered on blur
   - Flatpickr JS plugin (CDN) for date+time picker, styled brutalist, locale hr/en-US
   - Tailwind CSS "Competitive Brutalism" design system (0px border-radius, 2px black borders, Space Grotesk font)
   - AJAX search: JSON endpoints returning `{id, label}`, replaced table rows with stagger animation
   - Reusable autocomplete partial view via ViewData parameters
   - Animated delete confirmation modal (no native `confirm()`)
   - dotnet local tool manifest to pin dotnet-ef to v9 (fixes v10 tool vs v9 package mismatch)
   - TempData["Flash"] for success notifications
   - `initAjaxSearch()` shared JS helper in site.js

3. Files and Code Sections:

   **WebIde.Model/ (all modified to add soft delete)**
   - `Problem.cs` — added `public DateTime? DeletedAt { get; set; }` after CreatedAt
   - `User.cs` — added `public DateTime? DeletedAt { get; set; }` after RegisteredAt
   - `Tag.cs` — added `public DateTime? DeletedAt { get; set; }` after Name
   - `Organization.cs` — added `public DateTime? DeletedAt { get; set; }` after Description
   - `ProblemSet.cs` — added `public DateTime? DeletedAt { get; set; }` after OrderIndex
   - `Submission.cs` — added `public DateTime? DeletedAt { get; set; }` after PeakMemoryKb
   - `TestCase.cs` — added `public DateTime? DeletedAt { get; set; }` after Points

   **WebIde.DAL/WebIde.DAL.csproj** — upgraded EF packages from `9.*` to `10.*` (later reverted — actually the local tool was set to v9, packages stayed at 9.*)

   **dotnet-tools.json** (created at repo root) — local tool manifest pinning `dotnet-ef` to `9.0.16`

   **WebIde.Frontend/Models/** (all new):
   - `TagModels.cs` — TagCreateModel (Name [Required][StringLength(50,1)]), TagEditModel (Id + Name)
   - `OrganizationModels.cs` — OrganizationCreateModel/EditModel (Name [StringLength(100,2)], Description [StringLength(2000,5)])
   - `UserModels.cs` — UserCreateModel/EditModel (Username [Regex a-z0-9_-][StringLength(50,3)], Email [EmailAddress], DisplayName, UserRole, RegisteredAt DateTime)
   - `ProblemModels.cs` — ProblemCreateModel/EditModel (Title, Description, DifficultyLevel, TimeLimitMs [Range(100,10000)], MemoryLimitKb [Range(1024,524288)], AuthorUsername, CreatedAt, List<int> TagIds)
   - `ProblemSetModels.cs` — ProblemSetCreateModel/EditModel (Title, Description, CreatedAt, IsPublic, OrderIndex, OrganizationId [Range(1,MaxValue)], OrganizationName)
   - `SubmissionModels.cs` — SubmissionCreateModel/EditModel (SourceCode, Language, SubmissionStatus, SubmittedAt, Score [Range(0,100)], WallTimeMs, PeakMemoryKb, UserId [Range(1,MaxValue)], UserDisplayName, ProblemId [Range(1,MaxValue)], ProblemTitle)
   - `TestCaseModels.cs` — TestCaseCreateModel/EditModel (ProblemId, InputArgs, ExpectedOutput, IsSample, OrderIndex, Points [Range(0,100)])
   - `ExecutionResultModels.cs` — ExecutionResultCreateModel (SubmissionId, Stdout, Stderr, ExitCode, TimedOut, MemoryExceeded)

   **WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/** (all modified + 2 new):
   - `TagRepository.cs` — GetAll/GetById now filter `DeletedAt == null`; added Search(q), Add(tag), Update(), SoftDelete(id)
   - `OrganizationRepository.cs` — same pattern
   - `UserRepository.cs` — Search searches Username OR DisplayName
   - `ProblemRepository.cs` — same pattern
   - `ProblemSetRepository.cs` — same pattern
   - `SubmissionRepository.cs` — Search filters Language OR Problem.Title
   - `TestCaseRepository.cs` (NEW) — GetByProblemId(problemId), GetById(id), Search(problemId,q), Add, Update, SoftDelete
   - `ExecutionResultRepository.cs` (NEW) — GetById, GetBySubmissionId, Add

   **WebIde.Frontend/Program.cs** — added `using System.Globalization; using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Localization;`, registered TestCaseRepository and ExecutionResultRepository as scoped, added `UseRequestLocalization` middleware with hr + en-US cultures

   **WebIde.Frontend/Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml** — added Flatpickr CDN CSS + brutalist style overrides in `<head>`; added jQuery 3.7.1, jquery-validation, jquery-validation-unobtrusive, Flatpickr JS + hr locale, site.js before `</body>`

   **WebIde.Frontend/Views/Shared/_DatePicker.cshtml** (NEW):
   ```cshtml
   @{
       var fieldName  = ViewData["FieldName"] as string ?? "Date";
       var inputId    = "dp_" + fieldName.Replace(".", "_");
       var culture    = System.Globalization.CultureInfo.CurrentCulture;
       var isHr       = culture.TwoLetterISOLanguageName == "hr";
       var displayFmt = isHr ? "d.m.Y H:i" : "m/d/Y h:i K";
       // ...hidden input stores ISO value, text input shows locale-formatted display
   }
   // Uses flatpickr() JS to wire up picker
   // onChange stores ISO in hidden input: name=@fieldName id=@(inputId)_hidden
   ```

   **WebIde.Frontend/Views/Shared/_AutocompleteDropdown.cshtml** (NEW):
   - ViewData parameters: FieldName, Label, SearchUrl, InitialText, InitialIds, IsMulti (bool), Required
   - Multi=true: shows chips with × remove buttons, multiple hidden inputs with same name
   - Multi=false: single hidden input + text display
   - AJAX on keyup (debounced 280ms), animates dropdown scaleY(0)→scaleY(1)

   **WebIde.Frontend/Views/Shared/_DeleteModal.cshtml** (NEW):
   - Animated modal: `openDeleteModal(formAction, entityName)` and `closeDeleteModal()`
   - Backdrop fades in, box slides down from translateY(-40px)
   - DELETE button flashes red before form submit

   **WebIde.Frontend/wwwroot/js/site.js** (modified):
   ```javascript
   // 1. Blur validation: $(document).on('blur', 'input[data-val]...')
   // 2. Page-load row stagger animation (tbody tr opacity 0→1 with 35ms gap)
   // 3. initAjaxSearch(inputId, tbodyId, endpoint, rowRenderer) helper
   //    - debounced 300ms, shows skeleton loader, stagger-animates new rows
   // 4. Form focus animation: 2px→3px border on .brutalist-input focus
   // 5. Flash notification auto-dismiss after 3500ms
   ```

   **Controllers (all modified)**:
   - `TagController.cs` — added Create GET/POST, Edit GET/POST (ActionName), Delete POST, Search GET
   - `OrganizationController.cs` — same, kept [Route] attributes on all actions
   - `UserController.cs` — same, [Route("users")] prefix
   - `ProblemController.cs` — injected TagRepository + WebIdeDbContext; Create/Edit sync Tags (problem.Tags.Clear() + re-add from TagIds)
   - `ProblemSetController.cs` — injected OrganizationRepository; validates org exists on create
   - `SubmissionController.cs` — injected UserRepository + ProblemRepository; validates both exist on create
   - `TestCaseController.cs` (NEW) — route `problems/{problemId:int}/testcases`, scoped to parent problem
   - `ExecutionResultController.cs` (NEW) — route `submissions/{submissionId:int}/results`, on create links result to submission via ExecutionResultId

   **Views created/updated**:
   - `Tag/Create.cshtml`, `Tag/Edit.cshtml` — simple single-field forms
   - `Tag/Index.cshtml` — updated: flash message, Create button, AJAX search (client-side filter), delete modal, Edit/Delete buttons per card
   - `Organization/Create.cshtml`, `Organization/Edit.cshtml`
   - `Organization/Index.cshtml` — updated with CRUD buttons, AJAX client-side filter search, delete modal
   - `User/Create.cshtml` — includes Flatpickr datepicker for RegisteredAt, UserRole enum dropdown
   - `User/Edit.cshtml` — same fields pre-filled
   - `User/Index.cshtml` — updated: flash, Create button, AJAX search using `initAjaxSearch()`, Edit/Delete buttons
   - `Problem/Create.cshtml` — includes Tags autocomplete (IsMulti=true, SearchUrl=/Tag/Search), Flatpickr for CreatedAt
   - `Problem/Edit.cshtml` — same, pre-fills TagIds as comma-separated InitialIds
   - `ProblemSet/Create.cshtml` — includes Organization autocomplete (single-select), Flatpickr
   - `ProblemSet/Edit.cshtml` — pre-fills OrganizationName/Id
   - `ProblemSet/Index.cshtml` — updated: flash, Create button, AJAX search using `initAjaxSearch()`, Edit/Delete buttons
   - `Submission/Create.cshtml` — User autocomplete (SearchUrl=/users/search) + Problem autocomplete (SearchUrl=/problems/search), Flatpickr for SubmittedAt
   - `Submission/Edit.cshtml` — pre-fills both FK autocompletes
   - `TestCase/Index.cshtml` — table with Edit/Delete per row, Delete modal
   - `TestCase/Create.cshtml`, `TestCase/Edit.cshtml` — forms scoped to problemId
   - `ExecutionResult/Create.cshtml` — form scoped to submissionId
   - `ExecutionResult/Details.cshtml` — shows stdout/stderr/exit code/flags

4. Errors and Fixes:

   - **EF tool v10 vs EF Core packages v9 mismatch**: `dotnet ef migrations add` failed with `FileLoadException: Could not load file or assembly Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design.dll`. Fixed by creating a local dotnet tool manifest (`dotnet new tool-manifest`) and installing `dotnet-ef --version "9.*"` locally. Migration then ran with `dotnet tool run dotnet-ef migrations add AddSoftDelete`.

   - **Database password authentication failed**: `dotnet ef database update` failed with `28P01: password authentication failed for user "postgres"`. The connection string uses `Password=postgres` but the local PostgreSQL instance has a different password. Not yet fixed — migration file was created successfully and can be applied when correct credentials are available.

   - **`_DatePicker.cshtml` build error — `inputId_hidden` undefined**: Lines 34 and 44 used `@inputId_hidden` which Razor tried to resolve as a C# variable. Fixed by changing to `@(inputId)_hidden` in both the HTML `id` attribute and the JavaScript `document.getElementById(...)` call.

5. Problem Solving:
   - EF tool version mismatch solved with local tool manifest
   - Razor partial view parameter passing solved via ViewData dictionary (since partial views can't use @model parameters with Html.PartialAsync unless using PartialView() from controller)
   - Tag multi-select implemented as multiple hidden inputs with same name `TagIds` — ASP.NET MVC model binder collects them into `List<int>`
   - Autocomplete dropdown pre-filling on Edit forms: pass InitialText (comma-separated names) and InitialIds (comma-separated IDs) via ViewData
   - DB password issue is deferred — user needs to run `dotnet tool run dotnet-ef database update` with correct credentials

6. All User Messages:
   - "hey claude, please save this chat after we finish to the lab-4 folder in this project so i can commit it along with all skills and agent calls. we have lab-4.md in the root folder. Wee need to implement this lab in its entirety. Ask as many questions and plan as long as needed. we need to refine the ideas so were on the same page before implementation."
   - "Tool loaded." (multiple times after tool search responses)
   - "clear context and manually accept" (when ExitPlanMode was called — user approved the plan and wanted to proceed with implementation)
   - "implement it and manually ask for permission" (explicit instruction to implement the full plan, asking for permission before risky operations)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Apply DB migration (blocked by wrong postgres password — user needs to fix credentials in appsettings.json or start postgres with correct password)
   - Update `Submission/Index.cshtml` with CRUD buttons (Create, Edit, Delete), delete modal, and AJAX search using `initAjaxSearch()`
   - Update `Problem/Index.cshtml` with CRUD buttons (Create, Edit, Delete), delete modal, and AJAX search using `initAjaxSearch()` (the existing Problem Index has a complex filter sidebar and sorting — needs careful integration)
   - Save the chat/conversation transcript to the lab-4 folder as requested by user
   - Verify all CRUD flows work end-to-end once DB is accessible

8. Current Work:
   Immediately before this summary, the assistant was updating Index views to add CRUD buttons, delete modal includes, and AJAX search. The last completed file was `ProblemSet/Index.cshtml`. The pattern being applied to each Index view:
   1. Add `@await Html.PartialAsync("_DeleteModal")` at top
   2. Add flash message display block
   3. Add Create button next to page title
   4. Add AJAX search input
   5. Add `id="X-tbody"` to the `<tbody>`
   6. Add Edit and Delete buttons to each row's action cell
   7. Add `@section Scripts` with `initAjaxSearch(...)` call
   8. Change ACTION column header to ACTIONS

   The assistant had just finished `ProblemSet/Index.cshtml` and was about to read `Submission/Index.cshtml` to apply the same pattern — the file was read but the edit was not yet applied when the summary was requested.

9. Optional Next Step:
   The next immediate step is to update `Submission/Index.cshtml` with CRUD buttons, delete modal, and AJAX search — exactly the same pattern just applied to `ProblemSet/Index.cshtml`:

   ```
   // From the last work being done:
   // ProblemSet/Index.cshtml was just updated (completed)
   // Submission/Index.cshtml was READ but NOT yet edited
   // Then Problem/Index.cshtml needs the same treatment
   ```

   Apply the CRUD+search update to `Submission/Index.cshtml` (add `_DeleteModal` partial, flash block, Create button, AJAX search input with `id="sub-tbody"`, Edit/Delete buttons per row, `@section Scripts` block calling `initAjaxSearch('sub-search', 'sub-tbody', '/submissions/search', rowRenderer)`), then do the same for `Problem/Index.cshtml`.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/fbeleta/.claude/projects/-Users-fbeleta-Documents-WebIde-Web-ide/3212f87a-69f1-4c6f-bf60-5a49d511786a.jsonl
Continue the conversation from where it left off without asking the user any further questions. Resume directly — do not acknowledge the summary, do not recap what was happening, do not preface with "I'll continue" or similar. Pick up the last task as if the break never happened.

@if (hasAccepted) { check_circle } else if (problem.Submissions.Any()) { cancel } else { radio_button_unchecked }
@($"{problem.Id:000}_{problem.Title.ToUpper().Replace(" ", "_")}")
@foreach (var tag in problem.Tags) { #@tag.Name.ToUpper().Replace(" ", "_") }
@switch (problem.Difficulty) { case DifficultyLevel.Easy: EASY break; case DifficultyLevel.Medium: MEDIUM break; case DifficultyLevel.Hard: HARD break; } @rate% SOLVE

input.content:
# Lab 4 — Implementation Session Log

## Overview

Full CRUD implementation for all entities in the WebIde competitive coding platform (ASP.NET MVC, EF Core 9, PostgreSQL, Tailwind CSS brutalist design).

---

## What Was Implemented

### 1. Soft Delete — All Entities

Added `public DateTime? DeletedAt { get; set; }` to:
- `WebIde.Model/Problem.cs`
- `WebIde.Model/User.cs`
- `WebIde.Model/Tag.cs`
- `WebIde.Model/Organization.cs`
- `WebIde.Model/ProblemSet.cs`
- `WebIde.Model/Submission.cs`
- `WebIde.Model/TestCase.cs`

EF Core migration created: `AddSoftDelete`

```bash
# Apply migration (requires correct DB password in appsettings.json):
cd WebIde.Frontend
dotnet tool run dotnet-ef database update --project ../WebIde.DAL --startup-project .
```

Note: A local tool manifest (`dotnet-tools.json`) was created at the repo root to pin `dotnet-ef` to v9 (resolves tool/package version mismatch with .NET 10 SDK + EF Core 9 packages).

---

### 2. ViewModels — `WebIde.Frontend/Models/`

One file per entity with `XCreateModel` and `XEditModel` classes, all with data annotations:

| File | Models |
|---|---|
| `TagModels.cs` | TagCreateModel, TagEditModel |
| `OrganizationModels.cs` | OrganizationCreateModel, OrganizationEditModel |
| `UserModels.cs` | UserCreateModel, UserEditModel |
| `ProblemModels.cs` | ProblemCreateModel, ProblemEditModel |
| `ProblemSetModels.cs` | ProblemSetCreateModel, ProblemSetEditModel |
| `SubmissionModels.cs` | SubmissionCreateModel, SubmissionEditModel |
| `TestCaseModels.cs` | TestCaseCreateModel, TestCaseEditModel |
| `ExecutionResultModels.cs` | ExecutionResultCreateModel |

Validation annotations used: `[Required]`, `[StringLength(max, MinimumLength=min)]`, `[Range]`, `[EmailAddress]`, `[RegularExpression]`

---

### 3. Repositories — Write Methods + Soft Delete Filters

All existing repositories updated:
- `GetAll()` and `GetById()` now filter `WHERE DeletedAt IS NULL`
- Added: `Add(entity)`, `Update()`, `SoftDelete(id)`, `Search(q)`

Two new repositories created:
- `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/TestCaseRepository.cs` — scoped to parent Problem
- `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/ExecutionResultRepository.cs` — GetById, GetBySubmissionId, Add

Both registered as `AddScoped` in `Program.cs`.

---

### 4. Localization + Flatpickr Setup

**`Program.cs`** — added `UseRequestLocalization` middleware:
```csharp
var supportedCultures = new[] { new CultureInfo("hr"), new CultureInfo("en-US") };
app.UseRequestLocalization(new RequestLocalizationOptions {
    DefaultRequestCulture = new RequestCulture("hr"),
    SupportedCultures = supportedCultures,
    SupportedUICultures = supportedCultures
});
```

**`_Layout.cshtml`** — added CDN links:
- Flatpickr CSS (brutalist style overrides: no border-radius, black borders)
- jQuery 3.7.1
- jquery-validation + jquery-validation-unobtrusive
- Flatpickr JS + Croatian locale (`l10n/hr.js`)
- `wwwroot/js/site.js`

---

### 5. Shared Partial Views

#### `Views/Shared/_DatePicker.cshtml`
Reusable Flatpickr datepicker partial. Parameters via `ViewData`:
- `FieldName` — model property name (used for hidden input name/id)
- `Label` — display label text
- `Value` — current `DateTime?` value
- `Required` — whether field is required

Renders: text input (locale-formatted display) + hidden input (ISO UTC value for model binding). No native browser datepicker — uses Flatpickr with `enableTime: true`.

#### `Views/Shared/_AutocompleteDropdown.cshtml`
Reusable AJAX autocomplete partial. Parameters via `ViewData`:
- `FieldName` — hidden input name
- `Label` — display label
- `SearchUrl` — JSON endpoint (`/entity/search?q=`)
- `InitialText` — pre-filled display text (for Edit forms)
- `InitialIds` — pre-filled IDs (comma-separated for multi)
- `IsMulti` — `true` for tag chips (multi-select), `false` for single FK
- `Required` — validation attribute

Multi-select: renders removable chips, multiple hidden inputs with same name.
Single-select: one hidden input + text display.
Dropdown animates: `scaleY(0) → scaleY(1)` with `transform-origin: top`.
Debounced 280ms on keyup.

#### `Views/Shared/_DeleteModal.cshtml`
Animated confirmation modal (no native `confirm()`).
- `openDeleteModal(formAction, entityName)` — shows modal with entity name
- `closeDeleteModal()` — hides modal
- Backdrop fades in, box slides from `translateY(-40px)` to `0`
- DELETE button flashes red before form submit

---

### 6. CRUD Controllers

All controllers updated with full CRUD:

| Controller | Route Prefix | Special Notes |
|---|---|---|
| TagController | `/tag` | Simple name-only form |
| OrganizationController | `/organizations` | Name + description |
| UserController | `/users` | UserRole enum, RegisteredAt datepicker |
| ProblemController | `/problems` | Tag autocomplete (multi), CreatedAt datepicker |
| ProblemSetController | `/problemsets` | Organization autocomplete (single) |
| SubmissionController | `/submissions` | User + Problem autocompletes, SubmittedAt datepicker |
| TestCaseController | `/problems/{problemId}/testcases` | Scoped to parent problem |
| ExecutionResultController | `/submissions/{submissionId}/results` | Create + Read only (no Edit/Delete) |

Each controller has:
- `GET Create` + `POST Create` (with `ModelState.IsValid` check)
- `GET Edit` (ActionName="Edit") + `POST Edit` (ActionName="Edit")
- `POST Delete` (soft deletes, sets `DeletedAt = DateTime.UtcNow`)
- `GET Search` (returns JSON `[{id, label}]` for AJAX)
- `TempData["Flash"]` set on successful Create/Edit/Delete

---

### 7. Views — Create + Edit

Created for all entities:

| Entity | Create | Edit |
|---|---|---|
| Tag | `Tag/Create.cshtml` | `Tag/Edit.cshtml` |
| Organization | `Organization/Create.cshtml` | `Organization/Edit.cshtml` |
| User | `User/Create.cshtml` | `User/Edit.cshtml` |
| Problem | `Problem/Create.cshtml` | `Problem/Edit.cshtml` |
| ProblemSet | `ProblemSet/Create.cshtml` | `ProblemSet/Edit.cshtml` |
| Submission | `Submission/Create.cshtml` | `Submission/Edit.cshtml` |
| TestCase | `TestCase/Create.cshtml` | `TestCase/Edit.cshtml` |
| ExecutionResult | `ExecutionResult/Create.cshtml` | — (no edit) |

Autocomplete usage:
- Problem Create/Edit: `_AutocompleteDropdown` with `IsMulti=true` for Tags
- ProblemSet Create/Edit: `_AutocompleteDropdown` with `IsMulti=false` for Organization
- Submission Create/Edit: two `_AutocompleteDropdown` partials for User and Problem

Datepicker usage: Problem (`CreatedAt`), User (`RegisteredAt`), ProblemSet (`CreatedAt`), Submission (`SubmittedAt`)

---

### 8. Index Views — CRUD Buttons + AJAX Search

All Index views updated with:
- `@await Html.PartialAsync("_DeleteModal")` at top
- Flash message display block (`TempData["Flash"]`)
- Create button next to page heading
- AJAX search `<input>` wired to `initAjaxSearch()`
- `id="X-tbody"` on `<tbody>` for JS targeting
- Edit + Delete buttons per row
- `@section Scripts` with `initAjaxSearch(inputId, tbodyId, endpoint, rowRenderer)`

---

### 9. JavaScript — `wwwroot/js/site.js`

Four features added:

**Blur validation** — triggers jQuery Unobtrusive Validation on field blur (not only on submit):
```js
$(document).on('blur', 'input[data-val], select[data-val], textarea[data-val]', function() {
    var form = $(this).closest('form');
    if (form.data('validator')) form.validate().element(this);
});
```

**Page-load row stagger** — all `tbody tr` fade in with 35ms stagger on DOM ready.

**`initAjaxSearch(inputId, tbodyId, endpoint, rowRenderer)`** — shared AJAX search helper:
- Debounced 300ms keyup
- Shows 3-row skeleton loader while fetching
- Replaces tbody with rendered rows
- Stagger-animates new rows in

**Form focus animation** — `.brutalist-input` border widens from 2px to 3px on focus. Flash notification auto-dismisses after 3500ms.

---

## Known Issue: DB Migration Pending

The EF Core migration `AddSoftDelete` was generated but `database update` failed with `28P01: password authentication failed for user "postgres"`.

**Fix:** Update `WebIde.Frontend/appsettings.json` connection string with the correct password, then run:

```bash
cd WebIde.Frontend
dotnet tool run dotnet-ef database update --project ../WebIde.DAL --startup-project .
```

The app will not start correctly until the migration is applied (new `DeletedAt` columns are missing from DB tables).

# Lab 4 — Implementation Session Log

## Overview

Full CRUD implementation for all entities in the WebIde competitive coding platform (ASP.NET MVC, EF Core 9, PostgreSQL, Tailwind CSS brutalist design).

---

## What Was Implemented

### 1. Soft Delete — All Entities

Added `public DateTime? DeletedAt { get; set; }` to:
- `WebIde.Model/Problem.cs`
- `WebIde.Model/User.cs`
- `WebIde.Model/Tag.cs`
- `WebIde.Model/Organization.cs`
- `WebIde.Model/ProblemSet.cs`
- `WebIde.Model/Submission.cs`
- `WebIde.Model/TestCase.cs`

EF Core migration created: `AddSoftDelete`

```bash
# Apply migration (requires correct DB password in appsettings.json):
cd WebIde.Frontend
dotnet tool run dotnet-ef database update --project ../WebIde.DAL --startup-project .
```

Note: A local tool manifest (`dotnet-tools.json`) was created at the repo root to pin `dotnet-ef` to v9 (resolves tool/package version mismatch with .NET 10 SDK + EF Core 9 packages).

---

### 2. ViewModels — `WebIde.Frontend/Models/`

One file per entity with `XCreateModel` and `XEditModel` classes, all with data annotations:

| File | Models |
|---|---|
| `TagModels.cs` | TagCreateModel, TagEditModel |
| `OrganizationModels.cs` | OrganizationCreateModel, OrganizationEditModel |
| `UserModels.cs` | UserCreateModel, UserEditModel |
| `ProblemModels.cs` | ProblemCreateModel, ProblemEditModel |
| `ProblemSetModels.cs` | ProblemSetCreateModel, ProblemSetEditModel |
| `SubmissionModels.cs` | SubmissionCreateModel, SubmissionEditModel |
| `TestCaseModels.cs` | TestCaseCreateModel, TestCaseEditModel |
| `ExecutionResultModels.cs` | ExecutionResultCreateModel |

Validation annotations used: `[Required]`, `[StringLength(max, MinimumLength=min)]`, `[Range]`, `[EmailAddress]`, `[RegularExpression]`

---

### 3. Repositories — Write Methods + Soft Delete Filters

All existing repositories updated:
- `GetAll()` and `GetById()` now filter `WHERE DeletedAt IS NULL`
- Added: `Add(entity)`, `Update()`, `SoftDelete(id)`, `Search(q)`

Two new repositories created:
- `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/TestCaseRepository.cs` — scoped to parent Problem
- `WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/ExecutionResultRepository.cs` — GetById, GetBySubmissionId, Add

Both registered as `AddScoped` in `Program.cs`.

---

### 4. Localization + Flatpickr Setup

**`Program.cs`** — added `UseRequestLocalization` middleware:
```csharp
var supportedCultures = new[] { new CultureInfo("hr"), new CultureInfo("en-US") };
app.UseRequestLocalization(new RequestLocalizationOptions {
    DefaultRequestCulture = new RequestCulture("hr"),
    SupportedCultures = supportedCultures,
    SupportedUICultures = supportedCultures
});
```

**`_Layout.cshtml`** — added CDN links:
- Flatpickr CSS (brutalist style overrides: no border-radius, black borders)
- jQuery 3.7.1
- jquery-validation + jquery-validation-unobtrusive
- Flatpickr JS + Croatian locale (`l10n/hr.js`)
- `wwwroot/js/site.js`

---

### 5. Shared Partial Views

#### `Views/Shared/_DatePicker.cshtml`
Reusable Flatpickr datepicker partial. Parameters via `ViewData`:
- `FieldName` — model property name (used for hidden input name/id)
- `Label` — display label text
- `Value` — current `DateTime?` value
- `Required` — whether field is required

Renders: text input (locale-formatted display) + hidden input (ISO UTC value for model binding). No native browser datepicker — uses Flatpickr with `enableTime: true`.

#### `Views/Shared/_AutocompleteDropdown.cshtml`
Reusable AJAX autocomplete partial. Parameters via `ViewData`:
- `FieldName` — hidden input name
- `Label` — display label
- `SearchUrl` — JSON endpoint (`/entity/search?q=`)
- `InitialText` — pre-filled display text (for Edit forms)
- `InitialIds` — pre-filled IDs (comma-separated for multi)
- `IsMulti` — `true` for tag chips (multi-select), `false` for single FK
- `Required` — validation attribute

Multi-select: renders removable chips, multiple hidden inputs with same name.
Single-select: one hidden input + text display.
Dropdown animates: `scaleY(0) → scaleY(1)` with `transform-origin: top`.
Debounced 280ms on keyup.

#### `Views/Shared/_DeleteModal.cshtml`
Animated confirmation modal (no native `confirm()`).
- `openDeleteModal(formAction, entityName)` — shows modal with entity name
- `closeDeleteModal()` — hides modal
- Backdrop fades in, box slides from `translateY(-40px)` to `0`
- DELETE button flashes red before form submit

---

### 6. CRUD Controllers

All controllers updated with full CRUD:

| Controller | Route Prefix | Special Notes |
|---|---|---|
| TagController | `/tag` | Simple name-only form |
| OrganizationController | `/organizations` | Name + description |
| UserController | `/users` | UserRole enum, RegisteredAt datepicker |
| ProblemController | `/problems` | Tag autocomplete (multi), CreatedAt datepicker |
| ProblemSetController | `/problemsets` | Organization autocomplete (single) |
| SubmissionController | `/submissions` | User + Problem autocompletes, SubmittedAt datepicker |
| TestCaseController | `/problems/{problemId}/testcases` | Scoped to parent problem |
| ExecutionResultController | `/submissions/{submissionId}/results` | Create + Read only (no Edit/Delete) |

Each controller has:
- `GET Create` + `POST Create` (with `ModelState.IsValid` check)
- `GET Edit` (ActionName="Edit") + `POST Edit` (ActionName="Edit")
- `POST Delete` (soft deletes, sets `DeletedAt = DateTime.UtcNow`)
- `GET Search` (returns JSON `[{id, label}]` for AJAX)
- `TempData["Flash"]` set on successful Create/Edit/Delete

---

### 7. Views — Create + Edit

Created for all entities:

| Entity | Create | Edit |
|---|---|---|
| Tag | `Tag/Create.cshtml` | `Tag/Edit.cshtml` |
| Organization | `Organization/Create.cshtml` | `Organization/Edit.cshtml` |
| User | `User/Create.cshtml` | `User/Edit.cshtml` |

| TestCase | `TestCase/Create.cshtml` | `TestCase/Edit.cshtml` |
| ExecutionResult | `ExecutionResult/Create.cshtml` | — (no edit) |

Autocomplete usage:

- ProblemSet Create/Edit: `_AutocompleteDropdown` with `IsMulti=false` for Organization
- Submission Create/Edit: two `_AutocompleteDropdown` partials for User and Problem

Datepicker usage: Problem (`CreatedAt`), User (`RegisteredAt`), ProblemSet (`CreatedAt`), Submission (`SubmittedAt`)

---

### 8. Index Views — CRUD Buttons + AJAX Search

All Index views updated with:
- `@await Html.PartialAsync("_DeleteModal")` at top
- Flash message display block (`TempData["Flash"]`)
- Create button next to page heading
- AJAX search `<input>` wired to `initAjaxSearch()`
- `id="X-tbody"` on `<tbody>` for JS targeting
- Edit + Delete buttons per row
- `@section Scripts` with `initAjaxSearch(inputId, tbodyId, endpoint, rowRenderer)`

---

### 9. JavaScript — `wwwroot/js/site.js`

Four features added:

**Blur validation** — triggers jQuery Unobtrusive Validation on field blur (not only on submit):
```js
$(document).on('blur', 'input[data-val], select[data-val], textarea[data-val]', function() {
    var form = $(this).closest('form');
    if (form.data('validator')) form.validate().element(this);
});
```

**Page-load row stagger** — all `tbody tr` fade in with 35ms stagger on DOM ready.

**`initAjaxSearch(inputId, tbodyId, endpoint, rowRenderer)`** — shared AJAX search helper:
- Debounced 300ms keyup
- Shows 3-row skeleton loader while fetching
- Replaces tbody with rendered rows
- Stagger-animates new rows in

**Form focus animation** — `.brutalist-input` border widens from 2px to 3px on focus. Flash notification auto-dismisses after 3500ms.

---

The EF Core migration `AddSoftDelete` was generated but `database update` failed with `28P01: password authentication failed for user "postgres"`.

**Completed:**
- Soft delete (`DeletedAt`) on all 7 entities + EF Core migration created
